parsed_review.summary: CP00-659 (RP21 Admin Console failure-recovery permission/audit-binding descriptor slice, Risk B, 40 units: M04 "Secondary Workflow Slice" x20 and M05 "Permission And Audit Binding" x20, range RP21.P07.M04.S03-RP21.P07.M05.S20) is a sound, descriptor-only closeout candidate. Independently verified via Read/Grep/Glob: (1) Plan, next-pack-queue (live_latest_pack_id=CP00-659), manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and contract current_pack all agree on pack id, unit_count 40, range, upstream CP00-658, and next CP00-660/RP21.P07.M05.S21; deviation_from_plan:false is accurate and CP00-659 is the live cursor head. (2) validateAdminConsoleCp659Coverage enforces the 40-unit distribution (deliverables failure_recovery:22/security_audit:4/implementation:3/hermes_evidence:4/fixture:2/test:4/claude_review:1, micro_phase 20/20) against the plan pack. (3) The no-write/no-real-data boundary is structurally enforced, not merely asserted: freezeCp659Result injects ~30 top-level false-flags (writes_product_state, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_permission_decision, emits/persists_audit_event, executes_failure_recovery_runtime, persists_failure_receipt, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, persists_review_packet, leak_detected, permission_bypass_detected, ...) plus no_real_data/synthetic_only and the CP659 no-write attestation into the descriptor, case set, and Hermes/Claude packets, and validateAdminConsoleCp659FailureRecoveryPermissionAuditBindingDescriptor re-checks them plus nested contract/runtime/permission/audit/Hermes closure and the handoff target. (4) model.test.js carries three CP659 tests (coverage, descriptor boundaries, packets/contract projection) and command-evidence.json shows the full expected validation matrix (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check, build) all at exit 0 with writes_product_state:false and no_real_data:true. (5) Authority boundary respected: Claude is read-only and not promoted to final approval (promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claude_runtime_opened:false); Hermes appears only as a deterministic evidence packet/label/plan (hermes_runtime_opened:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime; the pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim (production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false). (6) Review hardening is healthy: readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, stdout 1248 bytes, valid JSON, no errors); prompt is 2502 bytes (<25600), contains no full diff/source, uses read tools only, and summary-only review is correctly prohibited for this Risk B security/review-evidence pack. The pending claude-review-result/construction-inspection/adjudication state is the expected pre-receipt posture and does not over-claim. One non-blocking P3 traceability defect found in the manifest's informational review_artifacts block. No P0/P1/P2 issues; blocks_goal_closeout is false (no runtime/enterprise-trust over-claim in this receipt).

findings.title: Manifest review_artifacts block references prior pack CP00-658 instead of CP00-659
findings.details: The manifest's top-level review_artifacts block points baseline_ref/readiness_ref/raw_output_ref/receipt_ref at artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-658/* rather than cp00-659/*. This is a stale copy from the CP00-658 manifest, whose own review_artifacts block self-references cp00-658 (confirmed at docs/closeout-packs/cp00-658/manifest.json:104-109), establishing that the convention is self-referential. The defect is NOT load-bearing: the closeout gate in scripts/validate-closeout-pack.mjs (validateHardenedClaudeReview) consumes manifest.pack_level_claude_review.review_receipt_ref, which correctly targets artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-659/review-receipt.json (manifest line 1502), and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-659/command-evidence.json review_artifacts also correctly targets cp00-659. Impact is limited to documentation/traceability: an auditor or tool that follows manifest.review_artifacts would land on the previous pack's review evidence (baseline/readiness/raw-output/receipt), which is misleading in a pack whose purpose is hardened review-evidence integrity. No functional gate, boundary, descriptor, test, or distribution check is affected.
findings.recommendation: Update the four paths in the manifest review_artifacts block from cp00-658 to cp00-659 so the informational reference matches the authoritative pack_level_claude_review.review_receipt_ref and command-evidence.review_artifacts. Optionally add a generator/validation assertion that manifest.review_artifacts.*_ref contain the pack's own id to prevent this copy-paste staleness in future packs.
